item.title: Achieving GPT-4 Like Results with Sub-10B Parameter Models
item.description: Introduction The realm of AI has witnessed a trend towards ever-larger models like GPT-4, with tens of billions of parameters. However, recent advancements have shown that smaller models, those with less than 10 billion parameters, can achieve comparable results. The key lies in focusing on two critical aspects: the quality of the training data and the implementation of innovative architectural changes. High-Quality Data is Crucial The effectiveness of a language model is largely determined by the quality of its training data.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Collin Heenan’s Projects Welcome to my projects page! Here, you’ll find a collection of my work in Economics, Computer Science, and Machine Learning. Each project reflects my passion for data-driven insights and innovative solutions. Domain-Specific Applications of Meta’s 7b Parameter LLama 2 Model Overview Description: This project involves the adaptation of Meta’s 7 billion parameter LLama 2 Model into a series of domain-specific models. The project showcases the potential of large-scale language models in specialized contexts.

item.title: About
item.description: Hello! I’m Collin Heenan, a driven second-year undergrad student studying Economics and Computer Science at Worcester State University. At the core of my academic pursuits is a deep engagement with Machine Learning and Quantitative Research. I’m particularly fascinated by the intricacies of Language Models, exploring how these sophisticated algorithms can process, understand, and generate human-like text. My focus lies in the technical aspects of Machine Learning - from neural network architectures to algorithm optimization.

item.title: Guide to deploy new posts to pages from Hugo
item.description: To create a new blog post navigate to the blog directory and just hugo new posts/<postname>.md Then edit the generated markdown file with what ever you want. Then save that new post and head and run hugo -t archie to generate the public files. Then cd into the public directory and add and commit those new changes to the MAIN branch.

item.title: Exploring the Versatility of Meta's LLama 2 Model in Domain-Specific Applications
item.description: Unleashing the Power of Large-Scale Language Models In the rapidly evolving field of machine learning, the implementation of large-scale language models stands as a beacon of progress and potential. My recent project, centered on Meta’s 7 billion parameter LLama 2 Model, epitomizes this advancement, showcasing the remarkable adaptability of such models across various domains. The Genesis of the Project The inception of this endeavor was rooted in a straightforward yet ambitious objective: to harness the sophisticated capabilities of Meta’s LLama 2 Model and tailor it for specific industries.
